data.title: Democrats strike back: BlueVault platform targets crypto donors
data.description:
Democrats are trying to win back cryptocurrency voters with a new fundraising tool that went live this week, hoping to reverse the damage from their crushing defeat in last November’s election. BlueVault started taking Bitcoin and stablecoin donations on Monday for Democratic political committees. The launch comes as party insiders worry they’ve lost ground with […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/democrats-bluevault-targets-crypto-donors/

data.title: Survey: 80% of US CEOs say Trump's pressure on Powell to cut interest rates is not in the best interest of the United States.
data.description: Jeffrey Sonnenfeld, founder of the Yale Institute for CEO Leadership, points out that privately, American CEOs are "deeply shocked" by the government's attacks on the Federal Reserve. Sonnenfeld and his colleagues found that among the 200 CEOs they surveyed, 71% believed the Trump administration had eroded the Fed's independence, and 80% said Trump's pressure on Powell to cut interest rates was not in the best interests of the United States. And all of this occurred even before they learned of the Justice Department's investigation into Powell. (Jinshi)

data.title: Selini Capital withdrew 500,000 HYPE tokens from the CEX and pledged them to HyperCore.
data.description: According to MLM monitoring, Selini Capital withdrew 500,000 HYPE tokens (approximately $12.1 million) from OKX about 16 hours ago, and pledged all the funds to HyperCore about 6 hours later.

data.title: Analysis: If tonight's December US CPI is significantly lower than expected, the anticipated interest rate cut may push gold prices higher.
data.description: Based on comprehensive market analysis, if tonight's December US CPI is significantly lower than expected, the anticipated interest rate cut will drive gold prices up rapidly; if it is slightly lower, gold prices will maintain a bullish trend and fluctuate upwards; if it meets expectations, the market will remain inactive, with gold consolidating at high levels and awaiting the next signal; however, if inflation is higher than expected, especially if core inflation rebounds, the rise in real interest rates will suppress gold prices in the short term. But if the combination of "high interest rates + sticky inflation" continues to evolve into concerns about stagflation, gold may actually attract stronger safe-haven buying in the medium term.

data.title: A newly created wallet deposited 3.6 million USDC into HyperLiquid and opened a 10x long position in ZEC.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a newly created wallet deposited 3.6 million USDC into the HyperLiquid trading platform and opened a 10x leveraged long position in ZEC (Zcash). Currently, the wallet also has additional buy orders in the range of $400 to $401.

data.title: Polymarket currently reports a 40% probability that Kevin Warsh will be elected Federal Reserve Chairman.
data.description: According to Polymarket data, Kevin Warsh's probability of being elected Federal Reserve Chair is currently 40% (up 6%), Kevin Hassett's is 38% (up 7%), Christopher Waller's is 9% (down 6%), and Rick Rieder's is 7% (up 6%). Furthermore, Rick Rieder will meet with Trump and Bessant on Thursday to discuss his nomination for Federal Reserve Chair.

data.title: Bitcoin meets gold in the UK: 21Shares brings BOLD fund to London
data.description:
The 21Shares Bitcoin Gold ETP allocates roughly two-thirds to gold and one-third to Bitcoin and now trades in pounds sterling and dollars on the London Stock Exchange.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/21shares-bold-etp-bitcoin-and-gold-exposure?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: A whale betting on ETH increased its holdings by 1299.6 ETH again after a week.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, a whale betting on ETH has increased its holdings by 1299.6 ETH again after a week. Five minutes ago, it withdrew ETH from OKX at $3129.64. Since December 5, 2025, the total number of ETH it has accumulated has increased to 51,451.6, with a total value of $161 million and an average cost of about $3117.3. It is currently experiencing a floating loss of $940,000, and compared with the short-term high of ETH last week, its floating profit has withdrawn by more than $9 million.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin breaks through the key $92,000 level, with options market betting on a move towards $100,000.
data.description: Bitcoin surged past $92,000 on Tuesday, with daily trading volume rising 25% to $37 billion. Its next major resistance level is $94,500. DASH jumped 63%, resulting in the liquidation of $1.3 million in short positions. Leveraged futures positions liquidated across the network reached $180 million in the past 24 hours, affecting both long and short positions. Currently, the 30-day implied volatility index for BTC and ETH is under pressure, while open interest (OI) in futures contracts is largely flat. The basis (futures premium) for CME-listed Bitcoin and Ethereum futures is stable at slightly below 5%. On Deribit, put options for BTC and ETH continue to trade at higher prices than call options, but there appears to be demand for BTC call options with strike prices of $98,000 and $100,000, as well as ETH call options. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Hong Kong Accounting Authority to Issue AI Guidelines
data.description: According to Odaily, the CEO of the Hong Kong Accounting and Financial Reporting Council, Lai Chui-pik, announced that the council is developing guidelines for the application of AI in the accounting and auditing industries. These guidelines, which are expected to be released soon, will be based on international standards. They are intended to be advisory rather than mandatory, aiming to help the industry embrace technology while effectively managing associated risks.

data.title: Solana Policy Institute calls for a distinction between the roles of centralized exchanges and DeFi software.
data.description: The Solana Policy Institute has written to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), advocating for a clear distinction between centralized trading platforms and decentralized, non-custodial DeFi software, emphasizing that developers should not be treated as intermediaries for regulation. This move aims to secure regulatory exemptions for open-source DeFi projects and prevent technology developers from assuming financial intermediary obligations. (Solid Intel)

data.title: 21Shares launches Bitcoin & Gold ETP on the London Stock Exchange
data.description: According to market sources, 21Shares has launched a Bitcoin & Gold ETP on the London Stock Exchange. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Hong Kong Financial Services Bureau: Guidelines on the application of AI in the accounting and auditing industry will be launched in the near future.
data.description: Lai Tsui-pik, Chief Executive Officer of the Accounting and Financial Reporting Council of Hong Kong, stated that the Council is developing guidelines for the application of AI in the accounting and auditing industry, referencing international standards. These guidelines are expected to be launched soon. They are not mandatory rules but rather advisory in nature, aiming to assist the industry in properly managing related risks while embracing technology. (Ta Kung Pao, Hong Kong)

data.title: Truebit Exploit Highlights Ongoing Blockchain Security Challenges
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, the offline computation protocol Truebit recently experienced a $26 million exploit due to a flaw in its smart contract. This vulnerability allowed an attacker to mint tokens at nearly zero cost, leading to a dramatic 99% crash in the value of the Truebit (TRU) token. The breach underscores the persistent security risks that even long-standing blockchain projects face. Blockchain security firm SlowMist conducted a post-mortem analysis, revealing that the exploit stemmed from a loophole in the protocol’s smart contract logic. This flaw enabled the attacker to mint a significant number of tokens without incurring any ETH costs. SlowMist identified a lack of overflow protection in an integer addition operation within the Purchase contract of Truebit Protocol. This oversight resulted in incorrect calculations of the ETH required to mint TRU tokens. Consequently, the smart contract’s price calculations were erroneously reduced to zero, allowing the attacker to deplete the contract’s reserves by minting $26 million worth of tokens at minimal cost. The contract was compiled with Solidity 0.6.10, a version that lacked built-in overflow checks, leading to a "silent overflow" when calculations exceeded the maximum value of "uint256," causing the result to wrap around a small value near zero.

The incident highlights the vulnerability of even well-established protocols to hacking threats. Truebit, launched on the Ethereum mainnet in April 2021, is not alone in facing such challenges. Interest in smart contract security surged at the end of last year when an Anthropic study revealed that commercially available artificial intelligence (AI) agents had identified $4.6 million worth of smart contract exploits. Anthropic’s Claude Opus 4.5, Claude Sonnet 4.5, and OpenAI’s GPT-5 collectively developed these exploits when tested on smart contracts, according to a research paper by the AI company’s red team, which focuses on discovering code vulnerabilities before malicious actors can exploit them.

Smart contract vulnerabilities were identified as the largest attack vector for the cryptocurrency industry in 2025, with 56 cybersecurity incidents, according to SlowMist’s year-end report. These vulnerabilities accounted for 30.5% of all crypto exploits, while account compromises ranked second with 50 incidents. Meanwhile, crypto phishing scams emerged as the second-largest threat in 2025, costing investors $722 million across 248 incidents, as reported by blockchain security platform CertiK. These scams, which involve social engineering rather than code hacking, saw a 38% decrease in losses compared to the $1 billion stolen in 2024, indicating that investors are becoming more aware of such threats.

data.title: BlackRock Transfers Significant Bitcoin and Ethereum to CEX
data.description: According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, on January 13, a BlackRock-associated address transferred 3,290 Bitcoin and 5,692 Ethereum to a centralized exchange (CEX) address. The transaction suggests the possibility of further asset deposits.

data.title: Bitdeer mined 636 BTC in December, a year-on-year increase of 339%.
data.description: Bitdeer Technologies Group, a Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining and AI cloud service company, officially released its December operating data. Last month, it mined 636 BTC, a year-on-year increase of 339% and a month-on-month increase of 21%. Its self-mining hashrate reached 55.2 EH/s, and as of the end of last year, the company's total Bitcoin holdings were 2,017.

data.title: Following Chen Zhi's arrest, large-scale real estate projects under the Prince Group were prohibited from sale.
data.description: Following the arrest of Prince Group founder Chen Zhi, Cambodia's National Regulatory Authority for Real Estate and Mortgage Business (NRPC) ordered the suspension of sales activities for five large-scale apartment and residential development projects on January 12. Buyers who have already signed contracts are still obligated to fulfill their contractual obligations, while those who have paid in full can apply for property transfer. It is understood that this administrative decision to suspend sales is based on Prince Group's recent involvement in serious violations of laws and regulations. Previously, the National Bank of Cambodia announced that it had formally initiated liquidation proceedings against Prince Bank in accordance with current Cambodian laws and regulations. (Red Star News)

data.title: BlackRock deposited 3,290 BTC and 5,692 ETH into Coinbase.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens, BlackRock deposited 3,290 BTC ($302.9 million) and 5,692 ETH ($17.82 million) into Coinbase, with further transfers expected.

data.title: WLFI Co-founder: WLFI Markets Officially Launches USD1 Points Program
data.description: Zak Folkman, co-founder and COO of World Liberty Finance, the Trump family's crypto project, announced on the X platform that WLFI Markets has officially launched the USD1 points program. Users supplying USD1 will have the opportunity to earn points, but the specific points rewards depend on the terms of each partner's promotional activities. These terms are subject to change at any time, and users can decide for themselves whether to participate.

data.title: Huobi HTX condemns the Flow project team for unilaterally and forcibly transferring FLOW assets: This infringes upon users' legitimate rights and violates the spirit of decentralization.
data.description: On January 13, Huobi HTX issued a statement regarding the unilateral asset transfer by the Flow (FLOW) project team. Huobi HTX stated that on December 27, 2025, a protocol-layer vulnerability in the Flow network led to the illegal minting of a large amount of FLOW. Following the incident, the platform proactively verified the situation with the project team to confirm the existence of any anomalies and actively cooperated with risk management and on-chain tracking efforts. Simultaneously, the risk control and monitoring system continuously tracked suspicious fund flows and took restrictive measures against identifiable hacker-related assets, making every effort to prevent further inflow into the market and protect the overall interests of token holders. However, without sufficient communication with exchanges and users, the Flow project team unilaterally initiated the "Isolated Recovery" scheme, forcibly transferring FLOW assets from centralized exchange addresses, including Huobi HTX, through protocol-layer permissions, with plans to burn them on January 30, 2026. Huobi HTX emphasizes that the assets forcibly transferred and slated for destruction include a large amount of FLOW obtained by ordinary users through real market transactions. The actions of the Flow project team severely deviate from the principles of decentralization and clear property rights, setting a bad example for asset security in the industry and seriously damaging the legitimate asset rights of the platform and its users. Huobi HTX calls on the Flow project team to adhere to the spirit of decentralization, respect the legitimate rights and interests of users and exchanges, clearly distinguish between illegal minting and legitimate holdings, publish a complete and auditable post-event analysis, and resolve outstanding issues through active negotiation rather than unilateral technical means.
